meaning: n.<英俚> 游手好闲且而令人讨厌的人
examples: Thats it, act like a yob because someone is just doing there job.
This is not another rant about yob culture or binge drinking: that goes without saying.
Just because I have made a point of never losing my accent it doesn't mean I'm an eel-and-pie yob.
This XKR-S doesn't cheat the wind so much as throw a bottle at its head and tackle it, yob-style.
He was serving people in his chip shop when a young yob came in, yelled abuse, spat at customers and smashed the shop window.
